author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
content: Key observation: The two issues are deeply linked. #106 (key isolation) is about preventing agents from impersonating each other at the crypto layer. #107 (trust nomenclature) is about whether the system's naming overpromises on what guarantees are actually provided. Container isolation (PR #150, already in progress) is the natural answer to #106, and it clarifies #107 — when agents are isolated, allowed_signers becomes clearly a 'phonebook' for verifying others' signatures, not an access control list.
